text: The abdominal team uses a standard endo-eye laparoscope to continue the total mesorectal excision (TME) dissection, while the transanal team has dissected cranially past the pelvic floor using a fluorescence-enabled pinpoint laparoscope. The near-infrared mode is activated, revealing a signal in the anterior plane of dissection, with no visible white light due to the penetration properties of near-infrared light.

The abdominal team temporarily removes their light source from the abdomen, then reintroduces it, revealing a stronger near-infrared light signal on the pinpoint display as the abdominal and transanal teams approach the join-up point.
